programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Karen Read's attorney grilled the state police sergeant who worked the investigation into the death of John O'Keefe. Her defense was nitpicking at what officers did and did not do, as her lawyers tried to raise reasonable doubt in the case against her. Sergeant Yuri Bukhenik explained when and how his team impounded the defendant's SUV. Prosecutors alleged this was the murder weapon that killed John O'Keefe.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: For a third day in a row a state police sergeant was testifying for the prosecution team.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Defense attorney Alan Jackson alleged that police manipulated video involving Karen Read's car.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The lead investigator in the murder investigation of a 16-year Boston police officer took the stand. Massachusetts State Police Trooper Michael Proctor told jurors how he initially got involved and took over this case. Jurors learned how Massachusetts State Police began its investigation into John O'Keefe's death. Just hours after he was killed, police interviewed Karen Read at her parents' home and seized her cell phone and car.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Michael Proctor was a key witness in this trial for both sides. For the State, he was in charge of the entire murder investigation. But according to the Defense, he was part of a massive cover-up which led to Karen Read on trial for murder. After getting initial statements from the defendant and the victim's friends, Trooper Proctor recalled that he and his team used search warrants to seize surveillance video and Karen Read's SUV.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: A State Trooper's Family Ties And Social Connections
programme.desc: Prosecutors brought up the family members of State Trooper Michael Proctor.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury learned about more text messages of a state trooper who was the lead investigator in the murder case of John O'Keefe. Michael Proctor was the lead detective in the investigation into what happened to O'Keefe. Karen Read's defense accused him of concocting a conspiracy to frame her. Prosecutors wanted to clear things up when it came to Proctor's text messages to his family, friends, and co-workers during the investigation.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: Unprofessional and regrettable. That's what Michael Proctor, lead investigator in the death of John O'Keefe, told the jury about some of his text messages. One of them called Karen read, the defendant, a whack job and retarded. Despite admitting to sending vulgar texts to friends and family, State Trooper Proctor did not waver on his conclusions that read intentionally killed her boyfriend with her SUV by hitting him with it and driving off, subsequently leaving O'Keefe for dead.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: It was sarcastic and a joke. That's how the lead detective put it when he was explaining away unprofessional and regrettable texts to family members, friends, and co-workers. Karen Read's defense team brought up those disparaging texts that Massachusetts State Trooper Michael Proctor sent while working this case. The Defense grilled him on the stand.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Conflicts Of Interest, Omissions, And Bias
programme.desc: This Defense team was not going to let up with its fiery cross-examination on the lead detective.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: An undetermined cause of death was an important element in Karen Read's defense.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The long cross-examination was not over yet for the lead detective on the stand.

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.desc: The lead detective on the stand answered questions under redirect. It came after a long cross-examination where the defense accused him of mishandling and corrupting the investigation into John O'Keefe's death. According to Trooper Michael Proctor, no one else but Karen Read was responsible for this homicide. Jurors would decide later if the lead case detective was truthful in his testimony. And, was he truthful in leading this murder investigation?

programme.title: Killer Or Cover-Up Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: More From The Massachusetts State Police
programme.desc: The lead detective wrapped up his testimony. Karen Read's defense team grilled him about his conduct during the investigation into John O'Keefe's death. Jurors would decide later if Trooper Michael Proctor's conduct during the investigation tainted the police's work? Next to the stand for the State was a higher-up in the Massachusetts State Police. He was the lead investigator's supervisor on this case.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A police officer accused of killing his estranged wife insists she committed suicide. Investigators say it was murder - that it was physically impossible for the woman to have shot herself.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police discovered the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents. It was the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1992, Laura Houghteling disappeared from her Bethesda home and was never seen again. Laura's bedroom was searched and forensic science was used to direct them a to prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend's house. She was never seen or heard from again. A tiny clue found years later revealed a tragic tale of drugs, romance and revenge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man tells police he shot an intruder who killed his wife. Forensic evidence disproves that story later on.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murder of an American tourist in a Mexican resort draws law enforcement resources in both countries.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a history of heart disease. Rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
